response.justification: Candidate has strong IC depth (15+ years), solid testing/TDD culture, and some developer-tooling work (test runner Trial, Fluent-Asserts, MCP code generators, CI/GitLab admin), but the resume shows no explicit people-management experience — no team leads, no direct reports, no 1:1s/performance reviews — which is a hard requirement (2 yrs people mgmt). Mobile-platform ecosystem exposure is also thin (brief Apple TV/iOS stint in 2015–2016). Title progression is IC-track (Senior/Staff Software Engineer), not management.
strengths: Deep testing and developer-tooling instincts (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos)
15+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and backend — strong technical breadth for hands-on guidance
Demonstrated technical leadership artifacts (workshops, talks, open-source platform GISCollective, CI/dev-ops setup)
concerns: No evidence of formal people management — no direct reports, hiring, or performance-review experience mentioned
Limited mobile-platform ecosystem experience (one short iOS/Apple TV engagement ~10 years ago)
Career trajectory is IC-focused; no Engineering Manager, Tech Lead Manager, or team-lead title in employment history
